MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Douglas M. McManamon of the law offices of Meyers Nave (510.808.2000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Alan C. Nolley of the law offices of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925.222.3400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Lend Lease (US) Construction Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Complaint of Plaintiff San Francisco College District, and as to all causes of action alleged against it in the complaint filed by plaintiff San Francisco Community College District is DENIED as to all causes of action. Assuming without deciding that the court can take judicial notice of the settlement agreement attached as exhibit A to the declaration of Alan Nolley, Lend Lease's motion must still be denied because the language of the settlement agreement is reasonably susceptible to the District's proffered interpretation of that agreement as releasing only claims relating to the claims relating to the claims asserted by Louie's California Chinese Cuisine in case 518143 and not releasing any of the claims asserted by the District in this case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
